claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 16 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions. Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children I'd like to ask about - Alex, who's 6, and Blake, who's 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have two children - Alex (age 6) and Blake (age 16).

Let me gather some more details to assess your eligibility. I'll ask about each child separately.

**First, do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't actually. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, this affects your eligibility.

**Which country are you currently living in?**

This is important because you may still be able to get Child Benefit if you live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of the armed forces posted overseas).

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Living in France may affect your eligibility, but there are some circumstances where you might still qualify.

**Are you or your partner working as a Crown servant (for example, a UK civil servant, diplomat, or member of the armed forces posted abroad)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us work as Crown servants. We're just regular residents here in France.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

Let me ask you another important question:

**Are you or your partner an EEA or Swiss national, or do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, we don't have any of those statuses.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information.

Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

Let me review the situation:
- You have two children: Alex (6 years old) and Blake (16 years old)
- You are living in France
- You are not a Crown servant
- You do not have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme or EEA/Swiss national status

According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally need to live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit. You may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant, but since you're living in France as a regular resident and are not a Crown servant, you would not meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Eligibility Assessment Results

I'm afraid **you are not eligible** for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake.

### For Alex (age 6):
While Alex meets the age requirement (under 16), you do not meet the residency requirement. Child Benefit requires that you live in the UK, with exceptions only for those living in certain countries as Crown servants. Since you're living in France as a regular resident, you don't qualify.

### For Blake (age 16):
The same residency issue applies to Blake. Even though 16-year-olds can qualify if they're in approved education or training, the fundamental requirement is that the claimant must live in the UK (with the same exceptions for Crown servants abroad).

**In summary:** The main barrier to your eligibility is that you're living in France and are not a Crown servant. If your circumstances change and you move back to the UK, you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time.

I'm sorry I couldn't give you better news. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

You may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant. Since the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirement and does not fall under the exceptions for living abroad, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 16 years old. The claimant would normally still qualify for Child Benefit if their child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirement and does not fall under the exceptions for living abroad, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
